understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to solve the level within a limited step budget by positioning the controllable piece(s) appropriately—typically by navigating around obstacles and, when relevant, getting a selectable piece onto the goal tile(s).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given in pixel/grid coordinates (with 3 pixels per cell). MOVE actions attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one cell in the chosen direction; moves could fail when blocked by walls/boundaries or other obstructing objects (and the 'movement_blocked' hint was only a heuristic, not a guarantee). CLICK switched control to another selectable located exactly at the provided [x, y] coordinate. Pushable blocks could be pushed if immediately adjacent in the movement direction, moving along with the selected piece when space allowed, while walls acted as blockers (with some wall/boundary behaviors needing to be inferred through attempted movement).
